question: Change into Passive: 'I had him repair my watch.'
options: I had my watch repaired by him.
I was had to repair his watch.
My watch was had repaired by him.
He was made to repair my watch.
answer: I had my watch repaired by him.
explanation: Causative verb 'Have' pattern: **Subject + have + Object + V3**. ✅

question: Change into Passive: 'It is time to take action.'
options: It is time for action to be taken.
It is time action to be taken.
Action is to be taken on time.
It was time for action to be taken.
answer: It is time for action to be taken.
explanation: Structure: **It is time + for + Object + to be + V3**. ✅

question: Change into Active: 'The trees were blown down by the storm.'
options: The storm blew down the trees.
The storm blows down the trees.
The storm had blown down the trees.
The storm was blowing down the trees.
answer: The storm blew down the trees.
explanation: Passive 'were blown down' (Simple Past) ➔ Active 'blew down'. ✅

question: Change into Passive: 'He likes people to call him Master.'
options: He likes to be called Master.
He likes being called Master.
People are liked to call him Master.
He is liked to be called Master.
answer: He likes to be called Master.
explanation: Infinitive object 'to call him' ➔ **'to be called'**. ✅

question: Change into Passive: 'Someone has to do this work.'
options: This work has to be done.
This work has to do.
This work had to be done.
This work is to be done.
answer: This work has to be done.
explanation: Infinitive 'has to do' ➔ **'has to be done'**. ✅

question: Change into Active: 'I was made to stand by the teacher.'
options: The teacher made me stand.
The teacher made me to stand.
The teacher makes me stand.
I stood by the teacher.
answer: The teacher made me stand.
explanation: Active 'make' takes a **bare infinitive** (stand), but Passive 'made' takes **to + V1**. ✅

question: Change into Passive: 'I saw him opening the door.'
options: He was seen opening the door.
He was seen to open the door.
The door was seen opened by him.
Opening the door was seen by him.
answer: He was seen opening the door.
explanation: Sensory verb + participle (opening) remains participle in passive. ✅

question: Change into Passive: 'Rose smells sweet.'
options: Rose is sweet when it is smelt.
Rose is smelt sweet.
Rose was sweet when smelt.
The smell of rose is sweet.
answer: Rose is sweet when it is smelt.

question: Change into Active: 'A ticket will be given to you by the conductor.'
options: The conductor will give you a ticket.
The conductor gives you a ticket.
You will give a ticket to the conductor.
The conductor will be giving you a ticket.
answer: The conductor will give you a ticket.

question: Change into Passive: 'Who taught you English?'
options: By whom was English taught to you?
By whom were you taught English?
By whom English was taught to you?
Both A and B are correct.
answer: Both A and B are correct.
explanation: Sentences with two objects (you, English) have two valid passive forms. ✅

question: Change into Passive: 'They suggest rebuilding the hospital.'
options: They suggest that the hospital should be rebuilt.
The hospital is suggested to be rebuilt.
Rebuilding the hospital is suggested.
Both A and C are correct.
answer: Both A and C are correct.
explanation: Gerund objects can use 'that' clause or direct subject transformation. ✅

question: Change into Active: 'Was the letter posted yesterday?'
options: Did you post the letter yesterday?
Did someone post the letter yesterday?
Both A and B can be correct.
Was you posting the letter?
answer: Both A and B can be correct.
explanation: Passive 'Was... posted' ➔ Active 'Did... post' with an assumed subject. ✅

question: Change into Passive: 'I must help the needy.'
options: The needy must be helped by me.
The needy must helped by me.
The needy should be helped by me.
The needy are to be helped.
answer: The needy must be helped by me.

question: Change into Passive: 'He wants me to buy a car.'
options: He wants a car to be bought by me.
He wants me a car to be bought.
I am wanted to buy a car by him.
He wants a car bought by me.
answer: He wants a car to be bought by me.
explanation: Pattern: **Subject + want + Object + to be + V3**. ✅

question: Change into Active: 'By whom has this mischief been done?'
options: Who has done this mischief?
Who had done this mischief?
Who did this mischief?
Who is doing this mischief?
answer: Who has done this mischief?

question: Change into Passive: 'They are looking into the matter.'
options: The matter is being looked into by them.
The matter is looked into by them.
The matter was being looked into.
The matter is being looked by them.
answer: The matter is being looked into by them.
explanation: Keep the preposition 'into' with the verb. ✅

question: Change into Active: 'One must keep one's promises.'
options: Promises must be kept.
Keep the promises.
We must keep the promises.
One keeps promises.
answer: Promises must be kept.
explanation: This is a rare case where the 'Active' form is usually the answer in reverse tests. ✅

question: Change into Passive: 'I am to do it.'
options: It is to be done by me.
It was to be done by me.
It am to be done by me.
It is to do by me.
answer: It is to be done by me.

question: Change into Active: 'Let the child be not teased.'
options: Don't tease the child.
Do not teased the child.
Let not tease the child.
The child was not teased.
answer: Don't tease the child.

question: Change into Passive: 'The judge suspected that someone had bribed the witness.'
options: It was suspected by the judge that the witness had been bribed.
The witness was suspected to be bribed by someone.
The judge was suspected that the witness was bribed.
Someone was suspected to have bribed the witness.
answer: It was suspected by the judge that the witness had been bribed.

question: Change into Active: 'This house was built by my father in 1990.'
options: My father built this house in 1990.
My father has built this house in 1990.
My father builds this house in 1990.
In 1990 my father was building this house.
answer: My father built this house in 1990.

question: Change into Passive: 'Did she buy a new laptop?'
options: Was a new laptop bought by her?
Is a new laptop bought by her?
Has a new laptop been bought?
Were a new laptop bought by her?
answer: Was a new laptop bought by her?

question: Change into Active: 'By whom were you taught Kannada?'
options: Who taught you Kannada?
Who teaches you Kannada?
Who has taught you Kannada?
By who taught you Kannada?
answer: Who taught you Kannada?

question: Change into Passive: 'People know that he is an honest man.'
options: He is known to be an honest man.
It is known that he is an honest man.
That he is an honest man is known by people.
Both A and B are correct.
answer: Both A and B are correct.
explanation: Two standard ways to handle clause passive. ✅

question: Change into Active: 'A lesson is taught to us by her.'
options: She teaches us a lesson.
She taught us a lesson.
She is teaching us a lesson.
She has taught us a lesson.
answer: She teaches us a lesson.

question: Change into Passive: 'Open your books at page ten.'
options: Let your books be opened at page ten.
You are requested to open the books.
Your books should be opened.
Let page ten be opened.
answer: Let your books be opened at page ten.

question: Change into Active: 'The thief was arrested by the police.'
options: The police arrested the thief.
The police has arrested the thief.
The police was arresting the thief.
The police arrest the thief.
answer: The police arrested the thief.

question: Change into Passive: 'She has not yet paid the fee.'
options: The fee has not yet been paid by her.
The fee was not yet paid by her.
The fee is not yet paid by her.
The fee have not been paid yet.
answer: The fee has not yet been paid by her.

question: Change into Active: 'Let the orders be carried out.'
options: Carry out the orders.
Carry out the order.
Orders should be carried out.
Let orders carry out.
answer: Carry out the orders.

question: Change into Passive: 'I saw him opening the box.'
options: He was seen opening the box.
He was seen to open the box.
Opening of the box was seen.
He is seen opening the box.
answer: He was seen opening the box.
